title: Price Elasticity Simulator
description: Industrial-grade tool to simulate price elasticity of demand, compute revenue impact, and identify hidden loss drivers. Aligned with ISO 9001, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC DC metrics.

inputs.label: Current Price per Unit
inputs.businessContext: The current selling price of the product in USD.

businessContext_i18n.en: The current selling price of the product in USD.

inputs.label: Current Quantity Sold (units/month)
inputs.businessContext: Monthly sales volume at current price.

businessContext_i18n.en: Monthly sales volume at current price.

inputs.label: Proposed Price Change (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage change in price (negative for decrease, positive for increase).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage change in price (negative for decrease, positive for increase).

inputs.label: Price Elasticity of Demand (PED)
inputs.businessContext: Estimated elasticity coefficient (typically negative). Absolute value >1 indicates elastic demand.

businessContext_i18n.en: Estimated elasticity coefficient (typically negative). Absolute value >1 indicates elastic demand.

inputs.label: Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Direct variable cost per unit (materials, labor, etc.).

businessContext_i18n.en: Direct variable cost per unit (materials, labor, etc.).

inputs.label: Fixed Cost per Month
inputs.businessContext: Total fixed costs allocated per month.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total fixed costs allocated per month.

inputs.label: Demand Shift Factor (seasonality/market)
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier to adjust baseline demand (e.g., 1.2 for 20% market growth).

businessContext_i18n.en: Multiplier to adjust baseline demand (e.g., 1.2 for 20% market growth).

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Quality of input data: low = ±20% error, medium = ±10%, high = ±5%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Quality of input data: low = ±20% error, medium = ±10%, high = ±5%.

rules.message: Price elasticity of demand must be negative (law of demand).
Price change percentage out of realistic bounds.
Variable cost must be less than current price to maintain positive contribution margin.
Current quantity must be greater than zero.

thresholds.message: Revenue decline exceeds 5% — consider alternative pricing strategy.
Profit margin below 10% — risk of unprofitability.
Extremely elastic demand — small price changes cause large volume swings.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
